all_candidates.topic: Are AI-driven silent layoffs justified in Indian IT?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Reports from July 9 and July 18-19, 2026, estimate 25,000-35,000 tech jobs could be cut in India this year due to AI adoption and productivity improvements.

all_candidates.topic: Was Sonam Wangchuk's protest removal justified?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from his protest site at Jantar Mantar by Delhi Police, widely reported on July 18-19, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should Delhi Police ban protests near Parliament?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Delhi Police announced on July 18, 2026, it will not allow a planned Parliament march on July 20, calling it 'illegal'.

all_candidates.topic: Does current tech hiring slump signal a wider economic slowdown?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Reports this week, including on July 18, 2026, indicate India's active tech job openings fell 14% month-on-month to a 28-month low.

all_candidates.topic: Should banks face penalties for delayed credit card closure?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New RBI credit card rules, effective July 15, 2026, mandate a ₹500 per day penalty for banks failing to close a credit card account within seven working days if there are no outstanding dues.

all_candidates.topic: Should credit card late fees only apply to outstanding?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New RBI credit card rules, effective July 15, 2026, mandate that late payment charges apply only to the outstanding amount, not the total bill.

all_candidates.topic: Should large merchants pay a fee on high-value UPI?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Centre is considering reintroducing a Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) of less than 0.5% on UPI transactions above ₹2,000 for large merchants, reported on July 18, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should the 28% GST on online gaming be reviewed?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Online gaming companies moved the Supreme Court on July 14, 2026, seeking a review of its May 27 judgment upholding 28% GST and retrospective tax demands.

all_candidates.topic: Should abusive language always be considered obscene?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Supreme Court recently ruled that using abusive language and swearing do not always count as obscenity under IPC (reported this week).

all_candidates.topic: Should UPI person-to-merchant limits be higher?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) raised the daily Person-to-Merchant (P2M) UPI transaction limit to ₹10 lakh for specified categories, effective July 18, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is mandatory photo ID for RailOne tickets fair?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Indian Railways introduced new rules from July 15, 2026, making photo ID mandatory for online general ticket bookings via the RailOne app and invalidating screenshots.

all_candidates.topic: Is a 3-day buffer for credit card dues sufficient?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New RBI credit card rules, effective July 15, 2026, introduce a three-day buffer before an account can be marked 'past due' to credit information companies.

all_candidates.topic: Does reducing train booking window benefit passengers?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Indian Railways reduced the advance reservation time limit for train tickets from 120 days to 60 days, effective July 16, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is the RBI Ombudsman scheme effectively protecting consumers?
all_candidates.news_anchor: An article this week highlighted that consumers can get up to ₹33 lakh compensation under the RBI Ombudsman scheme for bank grievances (July 18, 2026).

all_candidates.topic: Should all bulk train bookings be online-only?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Indian Railways updated its FTR rules on July 12, 2026, mandating all payments for booking entire coaches or trains to be online-only.

all_candidates.topic: Do new FCRA rules stifle NGO independence?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Rules, adopted June 22, 2026, and reported July 17, 2026, grant the government sweeping powers over foreign-funded NGOs.

all_candidates.topic: Should medical colleges directly appoint their own faculty?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The National Medical Commission announced this week that medical colleges will now decide their own faculty appointments and promotions (reported July 18, 2026).

all_candidates.topic: Should private space launches receive more government funding?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India's Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, putting multiple payloads into orbit on July 18, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should India prioritize hydrogen trains over other green transport?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India's first hydrogen train is expected to make its commercial debut soon, as reported on July 18, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is tokenising card data at merchants sufficient for security?
all_candidates.news_anchor: New RBI credit card rules, effective July 15, 2026, mandate tokenised card data at merchants for enhanced security.
